channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Vance visits Capitol Hill to urge senators to confirm Elbridge Colby for Pentagon No. 3 post
item.description: Vice President JD Vance praised Elbridge Colby, up for Pentagon’s No. 3 spot, as a "good man" and "honest man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU weighs $840 billion plan to 'rearm Europe' after US pauses Ukraine aid
item.description: European Union commissioner unveils new plan to spend $840 billion more on its own defense as U.S. support for Ukraine falters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance irks allies in UK, France with skeptical comments about Ukraine peacekeeping mission proposal
item.description: Vice President JD Vance has struck a nerve with allies after offering skeptical comments about a potential international security force for post-war Ukraine proposed by key allies Britain and France.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration to downsize Social Security Administration
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Elon Musk, the world's wealthiest person, who is also a senior advisor to President Donald Trump, is now setting his sights in recent comments on the Social Security Administration (SSA). "Social Security is the biggest Ponzi scheme of all time," said Musk.
item.dc:creator: Jay Dow, PIX 11

item.title: Waltz likens Zelenskyy to an 'ex-girlfriend' after Oval Office spat
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz compared Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to an “ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers get budget update that won’t include full scope of Trump federal pullbacks
item.description: Legislators are already weighing the ways they might have to bridge budget gaps if federal officials cut funding streams to Minnesota. A new economic forecast is released on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Inside Trump’s federal worker firings
item.description: Some of the removals have targeted a series of officials sure to conduct oversight of Trump’s actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Senate to vote on bill banning trans athletes from women's sports
item.description: The legislation asserts under Title IX protections for female athletes that gender is “recognized based solely on a person’s reproductive biology and genetics at birth.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: What you need to know about Trump’s address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Trump will lay out his second term agenda in an address to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday night. From talk on tariffs to the U.S. role in the world, here’s what to expect.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Seeing Washington change course on Ukraine, Taiwan ponders its own fate
item.description: Developments in the Ukraine-U.S. relationship have regularly made headline news in Taiwan lately. Many in Taiwan compare Ukraine’s fate to its own, as China continues to threaten an invasion.
item.dc:creator: Ashish Valentine, MPR News

item.title: Americans largely split on who should have birthright citizenship: Survey
item.description: The survey includes 51 percent of respondents who support the view that “all children born in the U.S. should automatically become citizens.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Demonstrators gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. Saturday to protest the automaker's billionaire CEO, Elon Musk, and his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules head of watchdog agency must keep his job, says his firing was unlawful
item.description: A U.S. District judge sided with Hampton Dellinger, who leads the Office of Special Counsel, in a legal battle over the president's authority to oust the head of the independent agency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: ACLU and other advocates sue to block migrants from being sent to Guantánamo Bay
item.description: The lawsuit says there is no legitimate reason to send migrants to Guantánamo because the U.S. has ample detention facility.
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer, MPR News

item.title: Crowds protest near Vermont ski resort where JD Vance planned vacation with family
item.description: WAITSFIELD, Vt. (AP) — Crowds protesting Vice President JD Vance the day after an Oval Office blowout over Ukraine lined roadways Saturday near a Vermont ski resort where he planned a weekend vacation with his family.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth directs civilian Pentagon workers to reply to OPM email
item.description: In a video posted to X by the DoD, Hegseth specified that only civilian workers should reply to the email and CC their "immediate supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans once maligned Medicaid. Now some see a program too big to touch
item.description: There's a mid-March deadline to avoid a partial government shutdown, and hesitation is surfacing among congressional Republicans about potential cuts to Medicaid.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: Demonstrators have gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. to protest the automaker’s billionaire leader, Elon Musk. Protesters are targeting Musk over his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Zelenskyy moves on after Oval Office blowout with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has thanked the American people and leadership and voiced hope for “strong relations,” a day after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with U.S. President Donald Trump that left many uncertain where the once staunch allies stood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
